title: Video: Fatima Bhutto Speaks At Harvard Kennedy School
summary: Fatima Bhutto, niece of the late Pakistani leader Benazir Bhutto, spoke to students and community members at the Harvard Kennedy School on September 29, 2010
body: Fatima Bhutto, journalist, author and niece of Benazir Bhutto, spoke to students and community members at the Harvard Kennedy School on September 29, 2010. Bhutto spoke about her latest book "Songs of Blood & Sword" and addressed current social issues in Pakistan. The event was co-sponsored by the Middle East Initiative at the Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, the Carr Center for Human Rights, and the Outreach Center for Middle Eastern Studies. In this video, Ms. Bhutto is introduced by Dr. Zil Yassine, a graduate student at the Center for Middle Eastern Studies at Harvard University. result.main_content_en: On September 29, 2010, journalist and author Fatima Bhutto, niece of former Pakistani Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto, delivered a talk at the Harvard Kennedy School. She discussed her book 'Songs of Blood & Sword' and addressed contemporary social issues within Pakistan. The event was co-sponsored by the Belfer Center's Middle East Initiative, the Carr Center for Human Rights, and the Outreach Center for Middle Eastern Studies. The video of the talk features an introduction by Dr. Zil Yassine, a graduate student at Harvard's Center for Middle Eastern Studies. The event is part of the institution's programming on diplomacy and international politics, with a focus on South Asia and the Middle East.
result.main_content_zh: 2010年9月29日，记者兼作家法蒂玛·布托——前巴基斯坦总理贝娜齐尔·布托的侄女——在哈佛大学肯尼迪政府学院发表演讲。她讨论了自己的著作《血与剑之歌》，并探讨了巴基斯坦国内的社会问题。此次活动由贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心的中东倡议、卡尔人权中心以及中东研究外展中心共同赞助。演讲视频由哈佛大学中东研究中心研究生齐尔·亚辛博士介绍。该活动属于该机构关于外交与国际政治项目的一部分，重点关注南亚和中东地区。
keywords_en: Outreach Center for Middle Eastern Studies
